title: Respiratory examination — consolidation findings
candidateInstructions: You are an FY1 on the acute medical ward. Mrs Dorothy Hughes, a 72-year-old woman, was admitted 24 hours ago with fever and breathlessness. Her CXR shows right lower lobe opacification. Please perform a respiratory examination and present your findings.
examinerNotes: Right lower lobe pneumonia with consolidation. Expected findings: reduced expansion right base, dull percussion right base, bronchial breathing and coarse crackles right lower zone, increased tactile vocal fremitus.

patient.name: Dorothy Hughes

hpi.character: Pleuritic right chest pain, productive cough green sputum
hpi.severity: Cannot walk to bathroom without breathlessness

systemsReview.cardiovascular: No chest pain other than pleuritic

examination.general: Flushed woman, tachypnoeic 24/min, using accessory muscles mildly
examination.respiratory: Reduced expansion right base. Dullness to percussion right base. Bronchial breathing right lower zone. Coarse crepitations right lower and mid zones. Vocal resonance increased right base.

investigations.name: Chest X-ray
investigations.result: Right lower lobe consolidation. Air bronchograms visible. No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax.
investigations.interpretation: Right lower lobe consolidation consistent with community-acquired pneumonia.

investigations.name: Sputum Culture and Sensitivity
investigations.result: Gram-positive diplococci on Gram stain. Awaiting culture — likely Streptococcus pneumoniae.
investigations.interpretation: Streptococcal pneumonia most likely. Sensitivities pending. First-line treatment: amoxicillin (but ALLERGY — must use doxycycline or clarithromycin).

patient.emotionalState: Tired and breathless but tries to be cooperative. Former nurse so understands the examination.
patient.communicationStyle: Short responses due to breathlessness. Cooperative.
hiddenConcerns: Will I need to stay in hospital long?
I know I have penicillin allergy — will that affect treatment?
doNotVolunteer: Penicillin allergy — only if asked about allergies
COPD history — only if asked about lung conditions

dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Respiratory rate, use of accessory muscles, chest shape, symmetry
Percusses both sides systematically, identifies dullness right base

dataGathering.criterion: Auscultation identifies bronchial breathing and crackles
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Auscultates both sides systematically, identifies bronchial breathing

dataGathering.criterion: Vocal resonance or tactile fremitus assessed
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Checks for increased VR/TVF over consolidation

clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: States pneumonia or consolidation as diagnosis
Selects clarithromycin or doxycycline (not amoxicillin) given penicillin allergy

interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Introduces self, gains consent, exposes chest adequately

references.source: BTS Guidelines for Community Acquired Pneumonia 2009 (updated 2019)

learningObjectives: Select appropriate antibiotic accounting for penicillin allergy
